solution:
```
import heapq
class KthLargest:

    def __init__(self, k: int, nums: List[int]):
        self.heap = []
        for num in nums:
            self.add(num)
        heapq.heapify(self.heap)
        while len(self.heap) > k:
            heapq.heappop(self.heap)

    def add(self, val: int) -> int:
        heapq.heappush(self.heap, val)
        if len(self.heap) > k:
            heapq.heappop(self.heap)
        return self.heap[0]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log k)
space complexity: O(k)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: We use a min-heap to store the k largest elements. In the constructor, we add all the initial elements to the heap and then pop the smallest k-1 elements to ensure the heap has k elements. In the add method, we push the new element to the heap and if the heap has more than k elements, we pop the smallest one. The heap operations (push and pop) take O(log k) time, so the overall time complexity is O(log k). The space complexity is O(k) as we store k elements in the heap.
